# shiki_flutter

> A syntax highlighter for Flutter, ported from Shiki in pure Dart. It tokenizes source code with real VS Code TextMate grammars and themes and renders it as styled `TextSpan`s, so highlighted code looks exactly like it does in VS Code, on iOS, Android, web, macOS, Windows, and Linux.

shiki_flutter is pure Dart out of the box (nothing to install), runs on every Flutter platform, and highlights off the UI thread by default on native/desktop. It bundles ~253 language grammars and 65 themes (plus 10 optional Pierre themes); you import only what you use and the rest is tree-shaken away. Render with the `ShikiCodeView` widget, the virtualized `ShikiCodeListView` for large files, or drop down to `codeToTextSpan` and raw `ThemedToken`s.

**Read the full documentation first.** The single file below contains the complete, authoritative docs (installation, rendering, widgets and their properties, themes, the full language/theme id lists, async highlighting, engines, web setup, configuration, bundle size, custom grammars, and limitations). Fetch it before answering questions about this package: it is compact, self-contained Markdown written to be read in one pass.

## Docs

- [Complete shiki_flutter documentation (Markdown)](https://shiki.birju.dev/docs.md): The entire docs page as one Markdown file. Fetch and read this in full for accurate API usage, the complete list of supported language and theme ids, code examples, and configuration. This is the most reliable source; prefer it over guessing.

## Reference

- [Package on pub.dev](https://pub.dev/packages/shiki_flutter): Versions, API reference, and install instructions.
- [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/BirjuVachhani/shiki_flutter): Repository, issues, and changelog.
- [Live documentation site](https://shiki.birju.dev/docs): The interactive docs, including the searchable theme gallery and language grid.

## Optional

- [Shiki (upstream)](https://shiki.style): The original TypeScript project this package ports; grammars, themes, and tokenization behavior match it.
